name: Assumptions Are Located
description: Every residual of kind 'assumption' must be locatable — it must carry a target or a non-empty related_artifact_ids, so a reviewer can find what depends on it.
rationale: An assumption the work relies on is only reviewable if a reviewer can find what it touches. An unlocated assumption ('I assumed something somewhere') gives the reviewer nowhere to point. Requiring a target or related artifacts keeps the residual surface navigable.

examples.passes: { kind: assumption, statement: 'upstream returns sorted results', target: { target_type: artifact, target_id: a3 } }
examples.fails: { kind: assumption, statement: 'upstream returns sorted results' } with no target or related_artifact_ids.
